title: Viola okuboi

intro: Viola okuboi, also known as Japanese pimpernel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the violet family (Violaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as skin irritation, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Viola okuboi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
